## Dominion Cheatsheet

### Skills

| Command | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `/dominion:orchestrate` | Drive the full phase pipeline (discuss → execute → review → improve) |
| `/dominion:orchestrate --dry-run` | Plan only — no code written, shows cost estimate |
| `/dominion:orchestrate --auto` | Unattended pipeline run with circuit breakers |
| `/dominion:quick` | Lightweight tasks, skip ceremony |
| `/dominion:validate` | Check config integrity |
| `/dominion:improve` | Review proposals, capture knowledge, create agents/skills |
| `/dominion:improve --agent` | Create a new specialized agent |
| `/dominion:improve --skill` | Create a new skill |
| `/dominion:improve --from <source>` | Import knowledge from external source |
| `/dominion:claim` | Merge Dominion with existing Claude Code setup |
| `/dominion:release` | Generate changelog, PR synopsis, publish release |
| `/dominion:status` | Pipeline status dashboard |

### Agent Roster

{{agent_roster_table}}

### Key CLI Commands

```
dominion-tools validate              # Check setup integrity
dominion-tools state resume          # Resume from last checkpoint
dominion-tools agents list           # Show active agents
dominion-tools improve list          # Show pending proposals
dominion-tools release prepare       # Generate changelog + synopsis
dominion-tools zone check <path>     # Check if path is in legacy zone
dominion-tools security scan          # Run security scanners, write findings
dominion-tools security findings      # List findings (filter by severity/status)
dominion-tools security finding <id>  # Show full detail for one finding
dominion-tools security track         # Compare findings across phases
dominion-tools metrics baseline       # Record recurring indicator measurements
```

### Directory Structure

```
.dominion/
  dominion.toml          # Project configuration
  state.toml             # Session state (gitignored)
  style.toml             # Code conventions
  roadmap.toml           # Phase roadmap
  agents/                # Agent TOML configs
  specs/                 # CLI spec, release spec
  knowledge/             # Domain knowledge files
  phases/                # Phase artifacts (plans, reports)
  signals/               # Inter-agent communication
  improvements.toml      # Proposal history
```

### How the Pipeline Works

Each phase runs through: **discuss** (capture intent) → **explore** (research codebase) → **plan** (architect tasks and waves) → **execute** (developers implement in parallel) → **test** (validate acceptance criteria) → **review** (quality check + improvement proposals) → **improve** (apply learnings, evolve setup). The Advisor guides you through each step. Use `--auto` for unattended runs with circuit breakers.
